name: Potential TinyTurla Activity
notes: This rule detects TinyTurla activity that uses a fake DLL called "w64time.dll". The Windows legit version is w32time.dll, which makes the malware less noticeable.
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following Audit.Object Load Success, System.Successful Registry Modification
when the event matches LOWER("Filename") MATCHES ('w64time*') OR LOWER("Registry Value Data") MATCHES ('.*?w64time*') AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Potential TinyTurla Activity on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Audit.Object Load Success, System.Successful Registry Modification
AND when the event matches LOWER("Filename") MATCHES ('w64time*') OR  LOWER("Registry Value Data") MATCHES ('.*?w64time*') AQL filter query
RED_list: Event description: Detects TinyTurla activity that uses a fake DLL called "w64time.dll".
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
